description_long: This is a hand tool featuring a robust wooden handle and a double-edged metal head. The handle is cylindrical with a natural wood grain texture, slightly curved for ergonomic grip, and appears to be made from a light-colored hardwood. The head is forged from dark metal, with two symmetrical, flat-edged blades extending horizontally from a central hub. The blades are wedge-shaped, with sharp, chiseled edges suitable for chopping or splitting. The head is securely mounted onto the handle, with the wood visible through the central eye of the metal. There are no visible markings, engravings, or labels on either the handle or the blade.
description: A double-edged axe with a light wooden handle and a dark, wedge-shaped metal head, designed for chopping or splitting tasks. The handle is slightly curved and textured for grip.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the double-edged metal head and the cylindrical wooden handle extending downward.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thickness of the metal head and the length of the wooden handle, with the blade edge profile visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the metal head and the end of the wooden handle, with the handle's grain and mounting clearly visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the other blade edge and the handle's length, similar to view 1 but from the reverse side.
attribute_handle_type: Cylindrical, slightly curved wooden handle with visible grain, smooth surface, and rounded end; cross-section is mostly circular.
attribute_blade_design: Double-edged, wedge-shaped steel head with flat, symmetrical blades and sharp chiseled edges; no beard or poll, utilitarian design.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with the handle passing through the central eye of the metal head.
